description_long: This object is a vintage corded telephone with a wooden base and a brass rotary dial. The base is rectangular with a slightly curved lower shelf and four short, outward-angled legs. The handset is made of brass and wood, resting on a cradle supported by two ornate metal arms, and is connected to the base by a long, thin black cord. The rotary dial is prominently featured on the top surface, with visible finger holes and metallic accents. The wood has a dark, polished finish, while the metal components have an aged, slightly tarnished appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century telephones, blending decorative craftsmanship with functional elements.
description: A vintage corded telephone with a dark wooden base, brass rotary dial, and a matching brass-and-wood handset connected by a black cord. The design features ornate metalwork and a polished, classic finish.
description_view_0: The rotary dial and the upright handset are clearly visible, with the cord trailing forward and the wood grain on the base prominent.
description_view_1: The handset is seen resting horizontally on its cradle, showing both earpiece and mouthpiece, with the cord extending to the left.
description_view_2: The rotary dial is in focus, and the handset is upright, with the cord trailing to the left and the wood's texture highlighted.
description_view_3: The handset is fully cradled, both earpiece and mouthpiece visible, and the cord is extended to the right, showing the symmetrical design of the base.
